characters.name: Colonel K
characters.description: Ask a Stupid Question...: From"Bandits, Beans and Ballyhoo":Colonel K:They don't call him El Loco for nothing.DM:Yes. Um...whydothey call him El Loco?Colonel K:Went off the rails at the age of 3. Borrowed Catchphrase: In"Bandits, Beans and Ballyhoo", DM is groping for an explanation on how El Loco the bandit snuck into his luggage when Colonel K abruptly says "Danger Mouse...shush." Electric Joybuzzer: One of the multitude of practical jokes DM and Penfold have to endure while attempting to get direction fromThe PranksterFunny Bone in"The Invasion of Colonel K". Exploding Cigar: In"The Invasion of Colonel K",Funny Boneforces cigars on DM and Penfold, despite their protestations that they don't smoke. The cigars immediately explode in their faces. "Fantastic Voyage" Plot:"The Invasion of Colonel K"centres around Greenback shrinking himself, Nero, and Stiletto down to microscopic size to literally get into Colonel K's brain and learn all the state secrets he knows. DM and Penfold are also shrunk down to microscopic size to go in after them.

characters.name: Danger Mouse
characters.description: AcCENT upon the Wrong SylLABle: One of DM's catchphrases was "You fiend", which he would inexplicably pronounce as "FEE-end." The Ace: DM himself; hisTheme Songeven tells you outright! He is ridiculously skilled at practically everything. Added Alliterative Appeal: Gets called "the White Wonder". All Just a Dream: In"Danger Mouse Saves The World...Again", DM and Penfold are in a room full of explosives with no way to escape, but just as the giant digital timer on the wall counts down to zero, it suddenly plays the alarm indicating an incoming call from Colonel K. Cut to DM on the sofa in his pillarbox jumping up in shock, revealing the entire episode up to that point to have been a dream. Animated Outtakes: in"Duckula Meets Frankenstoat", DM flubs his line twice when breaking in to confront Frankenstoat.

characters.name: Ernest Penfold
characters.description: Added Alliterative Appeal: Gets called "the Hopeless Hamster". Attack of the 50-Foot Whatever: In"Chicken Run", Penfold grows to giant size after jumping into a feeder full of Professor Squawkencluck's growth serum. He winds up being the episode's hero as a result, capturing Greenback and Stiletto in the process. Bad "Bad Acting":At the beginning of"The Four Tasks of Danger Mouse", Greenback reveals to Danger Mouse that he is behind Penfold's kidnapping, and shows him a live feed of Penfold in his cell. Penfold claims he is being treated well, but the fact that he makes this claim in an awkward, wooden monotone clearly indicates that he is just saying what he has been told to say (if the occasional prompting from Greenback wasn't enough of a giveaway):Danger Mouse:Penfold! Are you all right?Penfold:(monotone)I'm - being - treated - well.Greenback:Good, good!Penfold:(still monotone)The frost in my cell is very pretty, I am not hungry anyway, and, erm, erm...Greenback:And the beetles!Penfold:Oh yes.And the beetles are awfully friendly.In the last act of"The Duel"after Penfold has been hypnotized by "Madame Stiletto" — not just monotone, but also in "Madame's" fake Roma accent:Penfold:I want-a to go-a for a ride on-a Big Dipper.DM:Yes, yes, alright, we'reonthe Big Dipper!Penfold:An' I-a will not-a tell-a you... it is-a a trap!DM:Alright! Then don't!DM and Penfold do the opening of"Bandits, Beans And Ballyhoo"after the narrator storms off. Their dialogue is delivered hammily and slowly.DM:(gestures to the lift)Come on, erm... Penfold, my faithful assistant!Penfold:(clears throat)I'm coming, Danger Mouse. 'Cor, it's nice to be back in the Mayfair headquarters of the world's greatest secret agent!(he indicates DM, who mugs at the camera and shows off his badge)DM:Yes. Although that holiday in Mexico was most enjoyable.(Penfold shows the "Mexico" label on their luggage)Penfold:Yes. It certainly was, Danger Mouse. At the beginning of"The Four Tasks of Danger Mouse", Greenba

characters.name: Flying Officer Buggles Pigeon
characters.description: Big Damn Heroes: Buggles is this for both"Chicken Run"and"Ice Station Camel", respectively getting Danger Mouse out of a pickle by carrying him (and Penfold in the first case) to safety. Continuity Nod: When DM explains Buggles' role in helping destroy Greenback's world-slowing machine in"Ice Station Camel", he helps jog Penfold's memory about him by mentioning that he helped during the "giant chicken case". Got Me Doing It: In"Chicken Run", the character of Flying Officer Buggles Pigeon is a parody of the stereotypical upper class RAF officer, and his Verbal Tic of ending sentences with "what?" is both confusing and, apparently, contagious:Buggles:Good show, what?Penfold:What?Buggles:What?Penfold:You said "what".Buggles:No, you said "what".Penfold:(irritated)Becauseyou... said "what".Buggles:'' Hm? Jolly confusing, what?—->DM:What? (groans) Oh, this- I'm at it now!

characters.name: Agent  57
characters.description: Legacy Character: Agent 57's son is Agent 58 in the reboot. Inexplicably Identical Individuals: In"The Spy Who Stayed In With A Cold", Agent 57 turns himself into DM as DM himself is held hostage by the Motorized Mongols. Involuntary Shapeshifting: Becomes able to change into any character or animal whenever he sneezes in"The Spy Who Stayed In With A Cold". Master of Disguise: Agent 57, who appears as a worm in his first appearance and a polar bear in his second. According to DM, he's disguised himself as so many things over the years that he's forgotten what his original species was. Meaningful Name: It's been said that master of disguise Agent 57 was so named as a reference to the Heinz company and its "57 varieties."

characters.name: Professor Heinrich Squawkencluck
characters.description: Gratuitous Foreign Language: Uses german words like "nein" while speaking english. Herr Doktor: He's a German scientist. Hurricane of Puns: In"Turn Of The Tide", Professor Squawkencluck gets frustrated with an explanation of what's caused the ocean to submerge London and started screaming some amount of "Nein, nein, nein!" Whenever he did, Penfold would be nearby with a calculator, reading off the total of the "nines". Nephewism: P. Squawkencluck from the reboot is Heinrich's niece. Non-Indicative Name: He"s actually a mole.

characters.name: Baron Silas Greenback
characters.description: Added Alliterative Appeal: Gets called "the Terrible Toad". Be Careful What You Wish For: After DM causes the Mega-Brain computer to blow up in"Mechanised Mayhem", it eventually stops Greenback's travel device from attacking their HQ. Greenback wants a bigger and better computer, and the Mega-Brain computer crash lands on top of him and Stiletto.Stiletto:Oh, well...it-a could been worse, Barone.Greenback:How worse, you dolt?Stiletto:Well, ya coulda wanted the Q.E. II!(they both laugh, but then they hear a steamship horn; they glance up,groan "Oh, no!"as the Q.E. II lands on top of them) Big Bad: Danger Mouse's archenemy who was in almost every episode of the first five seasons. Borrowed Catch Phrase: In the original series episode"The Ultra Secret Secret", it's Greenback who tells Penfold to shush, and DMobjects. Call-Back: In"Penfold Transformed, Greenback plants Stiletto in a Penfold costume with Danger Mouse and refers to the mistake he made creating too many in"Tiptoe Through The Penfolds".

characters.name: Stiletto Mafiosa
characters.description: Accent Adaptation: In the original broadcast he had an Italian accent, but when the show was handed over to Nickelodeon in America, he was redubbed with a Cockney accent. The DVD set by A&E gives him back the Italian accent, which is quite a surprise to people who grew up on the Nickelodeon cartoons. A few of Stiletto's lines were changed as well- as seen in "Public Enemy No. 1" after DM suffers his amnesia-inducing bump to the head, there is this exchange:Stiletto:All right...I am surrendered!DM:Hmmm? Oh...how do you do, Mr. Surrendered.Stiletto:Eh? Is that-a English joke, signor? (In America with Stiletto's accent change, the line was changed to "Don't you know who I am, guv?") Be Careful What You Wish For: After DM causes the Mega-Brain computer to blow up in"Mechanised Mayhem", it eventually stops Greenback's travel device from attacking their HQ. Greenback wants a bigger and better computer, and the Mega-Brain computer crash lands on top of him and Stiletto.Stiletto:Oh, well...it-a could been worse, Barone.Greenback:How worse, you dolt?Stiletto:Well, ya coulda wanted the Q.E. II!(they both laugh, but then they hear a steamship horn; they glance up,groan "Oh, no!"as the Q.E. II lands on top of them) Conspicuous Trenchcoat: He always wears a classic trenchcoat and fedora, as befits a shady villain for a secret organization. Demoted to Extra: Alongside Greenback and Nero, Stiletto was in nearly every episode for the first five seasons. Starting with season 6, they began to appear less in favor of more villains for Danger Mouse to face. The Dragon: To Baron Greenback.

characters.name: Nero
characters.description: A Day in the Limelight: After Greenback's scheme is accidentally ruined before it even begins in "Nero Power", Nero is the one that antagonises DM and Penfold for the majority of the episode, with powers gained as a result of Greenback's missile's destruction. Demoted to Extra: Nero, alongside Greenback and Stiletto, was in nearly every episode for the first five seasons. Starting with season 6, they began to appear less in favor of more villains for Danger Mouse to face. Ridiculously Cute Critter: Despite his evil personality and affiliation, Nero is a very fluffy and cute little guy. Right-Hand Cat: Nero's a... furry caterpillar-thing, but it's obvious what trope he's invoking.

characters.name: Leatherhead
characters.description: Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Only ever appeared in the pilot, its remake and two other episodes of the main series. Fat and Skinny: He is a roly-poly contrast to the very skinny Stiletto. The Slacker: He spends the majority of his screentime reading a comic book instead of aiding the Baron's schemes.

characters.name: Count Duckula
characters.description: Attention Whore: Both versions of Count Duckula. Breakout Villain: He became popular enough to gethis own show. The Cat Came Back: Danger Mouse captures him in a sturdy crate, drops him onto a ship, and sends him away. Duckula reappears seconds later hitching a ride on DM's car with no explanation for his return. Foul Waterfowl: Duckula is a bloodthirsty megalomaniac and a recurring antagonist in the series. Freeze-Frame Bonus: In "Agent 58", a portrait of Count Duckula in his Spin-Off design can be seen at the villain convention.

characters.name: Grovell
characters.description: A.I. Is a Crapshoot: Fits this to a tee.Quark:Jangs, when I find the idiot what programmed you...

characters.name: The Narrator
characters.description: Added Alliterative Appeal: He frequently lampshades the show's frequent use of this:"London, a city shrouded in shadows. From Shoreditch to Shooters hill, from Shaftesbury Street to Shepherds Bush, shoppers shrink as shady shapes shuffle shiftily. Who can shatter the sinister shutters? Shout for the nation's shield!Shend for Dangermoushe!" Cliffhanger: The stories in Series 2-4 aired in five minute segments every weekday. The first four episodes generally ended with a cliffhanger over aTo Be Continuedscreen, and at the beginning of the next day's episode, the narrator would deliver aPreviously on…to remind viewers where they were the previous day. For example, Episodes 4 and 5 of"Close Encounters of the Absurd Kind"are bridged by a scene in which Penfold is sucked out of the airlock of a spaceship:Narrator:(at the end of Episode 4)Is Penfold destined to be the first Penfold togo where no Penfold has gone before?Can Danger Mouse get him back? Can our heroes escape from their alien captors? Will the guard lose his no-claim bonus on his hoverpod? And whereisBaron Greenback? To find out, tune in next time to Danger Mouse in"Close Encounters of the Absurd Kind"!(at the beginning of Episode 5)Colonel K warns Danger Mouse that the evil Baron Greenback is out to steal the Big Ear tracking station. But as he tries to seek out the evildoers, Penfold disappears in midair! About to be destroyed by Greenback's missile, Danger Mouse is sucked miraculously out of its path, and held prisoner by the strange creatures who idiotnapped Penfold. Mistakenly believing that they have been captured by the Baron, our heroes escape their captors, but while searching for a door to the outside, are once more parted! Will they ever meet again? Stay tuned for Part 5 of"Close Encounters of the Absurd Kind"! Description Cut: The Narrator frequently does this: From the opening of"Dream Machine":Narrator:Springtime in England, on a day unlike any other.(a sparkling green cloud floats over the Houses
